article_items.title: Anthropic scales Claude Mythos to critical infrastructure in 15+ countries | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Anthropic is expanding Project Glasswing, its security vulnerability program, and access to Mythos to 150 organizations across 15 countries - targeting critical infrastructure in power, water, healthcare, and communications where a cyberattack could affect 100 million people.

article_items.title: Industrial Software Leaders Build Secure, Autonomous AI Engineers With NVIDIA NemoClaw
article_items.description: Showcased at GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX, autonomous AI engineers compress weeks of simulation work into just hours.
article_items.excerpt: Showcased at GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX, autonomous AI engineers compress weeks of simulation work into just hours.
article_items.summary: Siemens is integrating NVIDIA NemoClaw and OpenShell into Fuse EDA AI Agent, a purpose-built autonomous agent that plans and orchestrates domain-scoped multi-tool workflows across semiconductor, 3D integrated circuit and printed circuit board system design. NemoClaw includes a choice of harness - meaning it can be integrated with various orchestration frameworks enterprises use to deploy and coordinate agents, such as OpenClaw and Hermes - as well as a model router and NVIDIA NeMo libraries for customization. Bringing together the PhysicsX platform, Microsoft Discovery and NVIDIA NemoClaw, the agent automates the full thermal simulation lifecycle for consumer devices such as Microsoft Surface laptops - from mesh sensitivity analysis and simulation data generation, through physics AI model training and optimization-loop execution, to continuous accuracy monitoring across the design exploration process. Watch NVIDIA CEO Jensen. Luminary is building a long-running AI engineer using NemoClaw to dramatically reduce the time and complexity of training AI physics models by autonomously orchestrating data generation, machine learning model selection, and training and re-training loops.

article_items.excerpt: Adding Custom Layers using the Python API (TensorRT >= 10.6) Appendix: Migrating Safety Runtime Code from TensorRT 8.x to 10.x TensorRT 11.0.0 Release Notes # These Release Notes apply to x86 Linux and Windows users, and ARM-based CPU cores for Server Base System Architecture (SBSA) users on Linux. This release includes several fixes from the previous TensorRT releases and additional changes. RHEL 10 / Rocky Linux 10 support : RPM and tar packages are now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10 and Rocky Linux 10. New Migration Guide content : The Migration Guide now includes a complete TensorRT 10.x to 11.x migration path with chapters covering the C++ API, Python API, trtexec ...
article_items.summary: Adding Custom Layers using the Python API (TensorRT >= 10.6) Appendix: Migrating Safety Runtime Code from TensorRT 8.x to 10.x TensorRT 11.0.0 Release Notes # These Release Notes apply to x86 Linux and Windows users, and ARM-based CPU cores for Server Base System Architecture (SBSA) users on Linux. The TensorRT Python bindings for Python versions 3.8 and 3.9 are considered deprecated starting with this release. New Migration Guide content : The Migration Guide now includes a complete TensorRT 10.x to 11.x migration path with chapters covering the C++ API, Python API, trtexec , Safety Runtime, IEngineInspector JSON output changes, and platform-specific guidance for NVIDIA DriveOS and Jetson/JetPack . IPluginV3 plugins that advertise a PluginField with a nullptr data pointer and length == 0 can crash inside the V3 creator dispatch. The OSS BERT plugin family is deprecated in this release and will be removed in a future release. As a workaround, rebuild the affected VC engines with a newer TensorRT 10.x release (10.2 or later) before deserializing them with the TensorRT 11.0.0 runtime on Windows.

article_items.summary: Unmetered Intelligence For Microsoft, integrating the Aion models into its Surface RTX Spark Dev Box (a developer-focused machine for maximizing compute and memory, housed in a striking black box) is one way to achieve what CEO Satya Nadella called "unmetered intelligence." That is the idea of moving away from deploying AI models in the cloud and instead enabling developers to run them locally on their computers. Along with Thinking One, Microsoft introduced models for text-to-image, image-to-image and voice. article_items.title: Alphabet Sets $80B AI Funding Goal
article_items.summary: The Google parent said in a statement on June 1 that the funds will support “investments in its world-class AI compute infrastructure to meet its unprecedented customer demand.” The fundraise will consist of a $30 billion public offering of Class A Common Stock and Class C Capital Stock, alongside a $10 billion private investment from Berkshire Hathaway -- building on the conglomerate's previous $4.3 billion stake in Alphabet it acquired in November. Alphabet is ramping up spending to try to close that gap. Google CEO Sundar Pichai previously warned investors the company is "compute constrained in the near term” – meaning it cannot build the necessary infrastructure to meet demand. “AI is driving an expansionary moment for Alphabet,” the company wrote in its statement. The development comes amid intensifying competition in the AI build-out race , with industry giants Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet and Meta estimated to be on track to spend more than $700B this year on data centers, chips and computing infrastructure. Alphabet is ramping up spending to try to close that gap, revising.

Alphabet is ramping up spending to try to close that gap, revising its capital expenditure forecast in April this year to between $180 billion and $190 billion, up from its previous estimate of $175 billion to $185.
An additional $40 billion at-the-market offering program for Class A Common Stock and Class C Capital Stock is expected to begin in the third quarter of 2026.
Google CEO Sundar Pichai previously warned investors the company is "compute constrained in the near term” – meaning it cannot build the necessary infrastructure to meet demand.
Alphabet revealed plans to raise $80 billion in equity offerings as part of an ambitious push to expand its AI infrastructure.

The development comes amid intensifying competition in the AI build-out race , with industry giants Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet and Meta estimated to be on track to spend more than $700B this year on data centers, chips.
During its Q1 2026 earnings call, Alphabet said it expects 2027 capital expenditures to increase significantly from 2026.
“AI is driving an expansionary moment for Alphabet,” the company wrote in its statement.

“The company is experiencing strong demand for its AI solutions and services from enterprises and consumers, at levels that are exceeding the company’s available supply, “By scaling its investments, the company seeks to.
Yet investment ambitions are outpacing the physical capacity to host the necessary levels of AI infrastructure .
Scarlett Evans is a freelance writer with a focus on emerging technologies and the minerals industry.
Scarlett also has a background in the mining and resources sector, with experience at Mine Australia, Mine Technology and Power Technology.

rag_rules: Make the business move concrete: who is involved, what changed, and why now.
Tie funding, partnerships, or revenue to product direction or competitive pressure.
Avoid hype words unless the article gives evidence.
Separate proven capability from claimed capability.
